user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the rear_bumper.
claim_status_justification: The user claimed a dent, but the image shows severe structural damage including a missing rear bumper and a crushed trunk lid. Because the damage is significantly more severe and extensive than a simple dent, the claim is considered a mismatch.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the front_bumper.
claim_status_justification: The user claimed a scratch on the front bumper, but image img_1 shows a significant dent and structural deformation of the front bumper, which exceeds the severity of a simple scratch. Image img_2 shows a different, undamaged car, which is irrelevant to the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the windshield.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 clearly shows a significant crack on the windshield, which directly supports the customer's claim of a stone-induced crack. Image 2 shows a different, undamaged windshield, but the presence of the damage in Image 1 confirms the claim is valid.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the side_mirror.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a cracked side mirror on a red car, which directly supports the customer's claim of damage to the side mirror. The mirror glass is shattered, confirming the reported issue.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard not met due to: wrong_object_part. The claimed part (quarter_panel) could not be evaluated.
claim_status_justification: The user claims damage to the rear bumper, but the visible damage in img_1 is located on the rear quarter panel, not the bumper. Image img_2 shows the rear bumper, which appears to be undamaged and fitted with an aftermarket guard.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard not met due to: wrong_object_part, damage_not_visible. The claimed part (headlight) could not be evaluated.
claim_status_justification: The image shows the side profile of a car, specifically the side mirror and door area. The headlight is not visible in this image, and no damage is apparent on the visible parts of the vehicle.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the door.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 is too blurry to confirm damage, but Image 2 clearly shows a physical dent on the door panel. This directly supports the customer's claim of a dent on the door panel area.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the hood.
claim_status_justification: The user claimed a scratch on the hood, but the image shows a vehicle with severe, catastrophic structural damage, including a missing front bumper, damaged headlights, and a mangled front end. The damage is far more extensive than a simple scratch, and the vehicle appears to be in a state of total loss rather than having a minor service-related mark.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the screen.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a laptop with a severely cracked display screen. This directly supports the user's claim that the screen is the issue following a fall.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the hinge.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 clearly shows the hinge area of the laptop is broken and physically separated from the screen assembly, which supports the customer's claim of a broken hinge. Image 2 shows the laptop from a distance, but the damage is confirmed by the close-up in Image 1.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the keyboard.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a laptop keyboard covered in water droplets, which directly supports the user's claim of spilling water on the keyboard area. The presence of liquid on the keys is consistent with the reported issue.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the corner.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 shows the laptop lid, while Image 2 provides a close-up view of a physical dent on the corner of the laptop chassis. The visual evidence directly supports the user's claim of a dented corner resulting from the impact.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the screen.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a MacBook Pro with a shattered display, which directly supports the customer's claim of screen damage. The damage is severe and consistent with the described incident.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard not met due to: damage_not_visible. The claimed part (trackpad) could not be evaluated.
claim_status_justification: The image shows the trackpad area of a laptop with a white circle drawn around a minor surface mark. This mark does not constitute physical damage such as a dent or crack resulting from an impact, and therefore does not support the user's claim of physical damage.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the package_corner.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a cardboard box with a physically crushed corner, which directly supports the customer's claim of a crushed corner on their delivery box.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the seal.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 clearly shows the seal of the package is torn and damaged, which directly supports the customer's claim that the seal side appeared opened. Image 2 shows an undamaged box and is irrelevant to the specific damage claim.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the package_side.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a large, dark, discolored stain on the side of the cardboard box, which is consistent with the customer's claim of water damage. The damage is visible on the exterior surface of the package as described.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard not met due to: damage_not_visible. The claimed part (contents) could not be evaluated.
claim_status_justification: The images show a box filled with packing paper. While the customer claims the item is missing, the images do not provide evidence of the item's absence or any damage to the package that would suggest tampering or theft.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard not met due to: wrong_object_part. The claimed part (box) could not be evaluated.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a dented metal food can, not a shipping box. While the can itself is damaged, the user specifically claimed the outside shipping box was crushed, which is not visible in this image.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is sufficient to evaluate the claim on the seal.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 clearly shows a torn seal area on the shipping box, which directly supports the customer's claim that the seal area was torn upon receipt. Image 2 shows an intact seal, which is irrelevant to the specific claim of damage.
